provisions.title: Title and Commencement
provisions.content: These By-laws may be cited as the Temeke Municipal Council (Property Rate) By-laws, 2001 and shall come into operation on the date of publication.

provisions.title: Interpretation
provisions.content: In these By-laws:— " Act " means the Local Government Finance Act No. 9 of 1982 ; " assessment " means the property rate assessment made under these By-laws; " Council " means the organ legally vested with the responsibility to exer­cise the functions of local 'governance' in the Municipality; " due date " means the date on which the rate required to be paid; " Chief Executive Officer " means the Officer responsible for the man­agement and control of the day to day affairs of the Authority; " property " means any building or structure within the area of jurisdiction of the Authority; " rate " means a levy on property charged under these By-laws; " Valuation Roll Phase I " means the rolls containing properties valued in 1994 in accordance with the provisions of the Urban Authorities (Rat­ing) Act , 1983 and includes supplementary roll thereon; " Valuation Roll Phase 11 " means the roll containing properties valued in 1999 in accordance with the Provisions of the Urban Authorities (Rat­ing) Act , 1983 and includes any supplementary roll thereon; " rateable property " means all houses within the jurisdiction of the Coun­cil which are in actual occupation and all improvements on, in or under any such houses;

provisions.title: 3 Assessment of rate
provisions.content: (1) Any person who is the owner of rateable property within the area of jurisdiction of the Council whose value is shown in the Valuation Roll Phase I or Phase II prepared in accordance with the provisions of the Urban Authorities (Rating) Act , 1983 duly gazetted in 1994 and 2000 respectively, shall be liable to payment of a rate being equal to zero point one five percentum of the rateable value shown in the Valuation Rolls; Provided that the minimum property rate payable shall not be less than the sum of ten thousand shillings. (2) Any person who is the owner of rateable property within the area of jurisdiction of the council whose property is not included in any Valu­ation Roll referred to in paragraph (1) of By-law 3 shall be liable to pay Flat Rate as specified in the Schedule of these By-laws.

provisions.title: Time to pay
provisions.content: The rate charged under paragraphs (1) and (2) of By-law 3 shall be payable to the council within thirty days of the date of such demand notice, commencement date being the 1 st day of the fiscal year.

provisions.title: Interest on unpaid rate
provisions.content: Where any rate remains unpaid after the date on which it becomes due and payable, interest at the rate of one percentum per month or part thereof shall be charged in accordance with the provisions of section 47 of the Urban Authorities (Rating) Act , 1983.

provisions.title: Destraint upon goods, chattels
provisions.content: If the rate remains unpaid after the expiry of the due date , the Chief Executive Officer shall proceed to distrain upon the personal goods and chattels in accordance with the provisions of section 26 of the Urban Authorities (Rating) Act , 1983.

provisions.title: Penalty
provisions.content: Any person who fails to pay the rate prescribed under these By-laws, whether such tax is legally demanded or not, shall be liable to a penalty of twenty five percentum per annum or part thereof of the amount of tax in respect of which such person is in default, or to imprisonment for a term not exceeding twelve months, or to both that fine and imprisonment.

provisions.title: Repeal
provisions.content: The Dar-es-Salaam City Commission (Property Rate) By-laws 1997 in so far as the Temeke Municipal Council is concerned are hereby revoked. Guest House Over 20 beds 100,000.00 Note: (1) Where the rateable ownership comprises of more than one of the above listed descriptions, composite of the appropri­ate flat rate taxes can apply to that owner and those various properties in the same cartilage. (2) (a) Prime Area means: All that area composing of the Business area and includes areas within the circumstance formed by an imaginary line drawn along central Railway line, Mandela Road cov­ering Nyerere Road, and Changombe Industrial areas, Mbagala Road, Kurasini to Bandari Road, Gerezani area back to Dar-es-Salaam port Railway siding. Any other area which the Authority can designate as such from time to time. (b) Secondary Area means: Any area other than the prime area.
